content:
The flour on my knuckles is no longer a shroud; it is just flour. 

For years, I thought the heat of the oven was the only thing keeping me solid, as if without the constant labor of the dough, I would simply dissipate like the steam from the baskets. I used the silence of my fading hearing as a cellar door, locking myself away where no one could ask anything of me that couldn't be answered with a
